behaviors.description: Primary translator produces grammatically coherent translation preserving meaning
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what a real neural translation model produces. Mocking the API would hide failures in the real model's grammatical coherence or meaning preservation. The test must call the real translation API (System-level infrastructure) and evaluate output quality using automated metrics (BLEU, METEOR) or reference comparisons. Cannot test 'preserves meaning' without a real model call—mocks would always return whatever we script them to return, hiding real quality failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real translation API with a test phrase in the source language, receive the translated output, and evaluate that the translation is grammatically coherent and semantically preserves the original meaning using automated metrics (BLEU, METEOR, semantic similarity) or comparison against reference translations.

behaviors.description: When translation API returns 503, service falls back without returning error to caller
behaviors.justification: While a Unit test could mock the translation API to return 503 and mock the fallback system, this would miss real failures in the fallback implementation: incorrect SQL queries, schema mismatches, broken wiring between error handling and fallback logic. Integration level is necessary to verify the actual fallback system works correctly when invoked, ensuring the end-to-end error recovery flow is sound. A pure in-process mock would hide whether the fallback is actually functional.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the translation API to return a 503 error, execute the service with a translation request, verify that the fallback dictionary system is consulted, and confirm that a valid translation is returned to the caller without any error response or exception.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for existing phrases
behaviors.justification: A Unit test with a mocked SQLite database would miss real failures: incorrect query logic, schema mismatches between code and database, data corruption, missing entries, or edge case handling. Only a real SQLite database (even in-memory) can verify that queries execute correctly, retrieve the right data, and work against an actual schema. Mocking always returns what we script it to, hiding real SQL failures.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a real in-memory SQLite database with known phrase-to-translation mappings, invoke the fallback lookup function for a known phrase, and verify that the exact pre-stored translation is returned correctly.

behaviors.description: Edit distance metric correctly computed and stored persistently
behaviors.justification: While edit distance computation is a pure function testable at Unit level, the storage aspect requires real persistence infrastructure. Mocking the database would hide write failures, data format issues, persistence errors, and retrieval problems. The complete behavior includes both computation and storage to real infrastructure (database, file, or log). A Unit test with mocked storage would always pass even if the real storage mechanism is broken.
behaviors.test_description: Generate two sample translations (primary and fallback with known differences), invoke the quality tracking system to compute edit distance between them, verify the computation is correct, confirm the metric is written to actual persistent storage (real database or file), and retrieve the stored value to verify it persists correctly.

behaviors.description: When both translators available, primary translation returned and quality comparison logged in background
behaviors.justification: A Unit test with fully mocked components could verify happy-path logic flow but would miss real failures in asynchronous behavior: if logging is incorrectly implemented as synchronous (blocking the caller), race conditions in background tasks, or error handling failures in the background logging task. Integration level is needed to verify that logging truly executes in the background without blocking the caller's response and that the background task completes correctly. Pure in-process mocks executing synchronously would hide blocking failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the service with both translators available (using mocked primary and fallback APIs with known outputs), verify the primary translation is returned immediately to the caller, confirm logging of the quality comparison is initiated, and verify via async testing that the logging task completes in the background without blocking the caller's response and that logged metrics are eventually persisted.
